lyrics:
Oh, oh, yeah, yeah, yeah
What you want will come your way

Sometimes the road feels long and rough
And every step just ain’t enough
But the sun still shines behind the rain
Every storm will fade, the joy remains
Lift your head, don’t lose your sight
What you want is within your light

What you want, it’ll come in time
Like the river flows, like the stars align
Don’t you worry, it will be ok
What you want will come your way

The world will spin, no matter how
Keep your faith, just be here now
The seeds you plant will one day grow
The love you give will always show
One step forward, you’re on your way
What you want is here to stay

What you want, it’ll come in time
Like the river flows, like the stars align
Don’t you worry, it will be ok
What you want will come your way

(Hummed melody, softly sung)
Oh, oh, don’t fear the night
The dawn is near, the world’s alright
Trust the rhythm, trust the flow
What you want, let the feeling grow

What you want, it’ll come in time
Like the river flows, like the stars align
Don’t you worry, it will be ok
What you want will come your way

Oh, oh, yeah, yeah, yeah
What you want will come your way
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track features a warm, organic texture led by a strummed acoustic guitar and a sincere male lead vocal, creating an atmosphere that is both hopeful and encouraging. The song has a mid-level energy that builds dynamically, starting with a gentle, reflective verse and swelling into a full-band, driving feel for the anthemic chorus. The production is clean and well-balanced, allowing ample space for the vocals to sit clearly on top of the rhythm section and supporting harmonies without sounding crowded. A defining quality of the track is the powerful transition into the hook at 0:48, where the full arrangement kicks in, solidifying the song's uplifting and motivational message.

review.review_text: A waltz-time anthem in F major that earns its optimism the old-fashioned way: a verse that actually sets something up before the chorus pays it off. The full-band kick at 0:48 lands with the kind of lift you get from early Tom Petty, and lines like 'the seeds you plant will one day grow' stay on the honest side of inspirational without tipping into greeting card. At 2:39 it knows exactly when to leave.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'the seeds you plant will one day grow'
